html:
[403]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 女凡事能先知 - The woman could foresee everything.
  • 遇有疑难与议，无不剖 - When faced with difficult questions and discussions, she could always resolve them. The verb '剖' (pōu) means to dissect or resolve, often used metaphorically for solving complex problems.
  • 大劫将至，为之奈何 - A great calamity is coming, what can be done about it? The phrase '大劫' (dà jié) refers to a major disaster or catastrophe.
  • 余悉无恙 - I am all well. '悉' (xī) means completely, and '无恙' (wú yàng) means safe or unharmed.
  • 独二公子可虑 - Only the second son is a concern. '独' (dú) means only, and '可虑' (kě lǜ) means something to be worried about.
  • 此处不久将为战场 - This place will soon become a battlefield. '战场' (zhànchǎng) means battlefield.
  • 君当求差远去，庶免于难 - You should request to be transferred far away, to avoid trouble. '求差' (qiú chāi) means to request a transfer, '远去' (yuǎn qù) means to go far away, and '庶免于难' (shù miǎn yú nán) means to hopefully avoid difficulties.
  • 道里辽远，闻者吊之 - The journey is long and distant, those who heard about it mourned for him. '道里' (dào lǐ) means distance, '辽远' (liáo yuǎn) means long and distant, '闻者' (wén zhě) means those who heard, and '吊之' (diào zhī) means to mourn for someone.
  • 而女独贺 - But the woman alone congratulated him. '独' (dú) means alone, and '贺' (hè) means to congratulate.
  • 无何，姜瓖叛，汾州没为贼窟 - Not long after, Jiang Xiang rebelled, and Fen Prefecture became a nest of bandits. '无何' (wú hé) means not long after, '姜瓖叛' (jiāng xiāng pàn) means Jiang Xiang rebelled, '汾州' (fén zhōu) means Fen Prefecture, '没为' (méi wéi) means became, and '贼窟' (zéi kū) means nest of bandits.
  • 刘仲子自山东来，适遭其变，遂被其害 - Liu Zhongzi came from Shandong and happened to encounter the rebellion, thus being harmed by it. '适' (shì) means happened to, '遭其变' (zāo qí biàn) means encountered the change or rebellion, and '遂被其害' (suì bèi qí hài) means thus was harmed by it.
  • 城陷，官僚皆罹干难 - The city was captured, and all the officials suffered great difficulties. '城陷' (chéng xiàn) means the city was captured, '官僚' (guān liáo) means officials, '皆' (jiē) means all, '罹干难' (lí gàn nán) means suffered great difficulties.
  • 惟刘以公出得免 - Only Liu, due to his official duties, was spared. '惟' (wéi) means only, '以公出' (yǐ gōng chū) means due to his official duties, and '得免' (dé miǎn) means was spared.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姜瓖叛 - Refers to the rebellion led by Jiang Xiang, a historical event during the late Ming and early Qing dynasties.
  • 汾州 - Refers to Fen Prefecture, a region in Shanxi Province, China.
  • 贼窟 - Refers to a place where bandits or rebels gather, often used to describe a stronghold or base of operations.

Proper Nouns

  • 姜瓖 - Jiang Xiang, a historical figure who led a rebellion.
  • 汾州 - Fen Prefecture, a region in Shanxi Province, China.
  • 刘仲子 - Liu Zhongzi, a character in the story.

[404]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 盗平 - Bandits were pacified; he returned home.
  • 寻以大案桂误 - Soon after a major case involving Gui, which could mean 'mistake in the case of Gui' or 'Gui's error.'
  • 贫至饔飧不给 - Poor to the point of not having enough food.
  • 当道者又多所需索 - Officials often made many demands.
  • 因而窘忧欲死 - He was so distressed and worried that he wanted to die.
  • 床下三千金 - Three thousand gold pieces hidden under the bed.
  • 可资用度 - Can be used for expenses.
  • 窃之何处 - Where did you steal it?
  • 天下无主之物取之不尽 - There are things in the world without an owner that can be taken endlessly.
  • 锐身自任之 - To take responsibility for it.
  • 遗襁褓子及未亡人 - Left behind an infant and a widow.
  • 以时恤诸其家 - To provide for them at the right time.
  • 每得升斗必析而二之 - Every time he gets a small amount, he always divides it into two.
  • 夏妻子赖以活 - Xia's wife and children relied on it to live.
  • 士大夫益贤乐 - Scholars and officials esteemed Le even more.
  • 恒产无多 - He had not much permanent property.
  • 又代夏生忧内顾 - Also worried about Xia's son and family.
  • 家计日蹙 - His family finances were daily shrinking.
  • 文如平子尚碌碌以没 - Like Pingzi, still ordinary and died.
  • 而况于我 - Let alone me.
  • 人生富贵须及时 - One must seek wealth and honor in time.
  • 戚戚终岁 - Anxious throughout the year.
  • 恐先狗马填沟壑 - Fear of dying before dogs and horses.
  • 负此生矣 - To disappoint this life.
  • 不如早改图也 - Not as good as to change plans early.
  • 于是去读而贾 - Therefore, he stopped reading and started trading.
  • 操业半年 - Engaged in business for half a year.
  • 家资小泰 - His family wealth slightly prospered.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 盗平 - Pacification of bandits or robbers.
  • 大案桂误 - Major case involving Gui, possibly a historical or fictional figure.
  • 饔飧不给 - Lack of basic necessities, specifically food.
  • 当道者 - Officials or those in power.
  • 床下三千金 - Large sum of money hidden under the bed.
  • 窃之何处 - Act of stealing and the location of the stolen goods.
  • 天下无主之物取之不尽 - Resources in the world that can be taken without limit.
  • 锐身自任之 - Taking responsibility for someone else's affairs.
  • 遗襁褓子及未亡人 - Leaving behind an infant and a widow.
  • 以时恤诸其家 - Providing for someone's family at the right time.
  • 每得升斗必析而二之 - Dividing a small amount of resources equally.
  • 夏妻子赖以活 - Xia's wife and children relying on someone's help to survive.
  • 士大夫益贤乐 - Scholars and officials respecting Le more.
  • 恒产无多 - Having limited permanent property.
  • 又代夏生忧内顾 - Worrying about Xia's son and family.
  • 家计日蹙 - Daily financial difficulties.
  • 文如平子尚碌碌以没 - Pingzi, possibly a historical or fictional figure, who lived an ordinary life and died.
  • 而况于我 - One's own situation is even worse.
  • 人生富贵须及时 - Seeking wealth and honor in time.
  • 戚戚终岁 - Being anxious throughout the year.
  • 恐先狗马填沟壑 - Fear of dying before dogs and horses.
  • 负此生矣 - Disappointing one's life.
  • 不如早改图也 - Changing one's plans early.
  • 于是去读而贾 - Stopping reading and starting trading.
  • 操业半年 - Engaging in business for half a year.
  • 家资小泰 - Slight improvement in family wealth.

[405]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一日客金陵 - Means 'one day, a guest in Jinling (Nanjing),' setting the scene for the narrative.
  • 休于旅舍 - Means 'rested at an inn,' indicating the setting where the encounter takes place.
  • 颀然而长 - Describes a person as tall and slender, emphasizing physical appearance.
  • 筋骨隆起 - Means 'muscles and bones prominently protruding,' further describing the person's physique.
  • 彷徨坐侧 - Means 'wandering around the side,' suggesting restlessness or uncertainty.
  • 色黯淡有戚容 - Means 'with a dull and sorrowful expression,' indicating the person's emotional state.
  • 欲得食耶 - Means 'do you want food?' a direct question to the person.
  • 以手掬啖 - Means 'eating with hands,' indicating a manner of eating.
  • 顷刻已尽 - Means 'in a moment, finished,' emphasizing the speed of consumption.
  • 益以兼人之馔 - Means 'adding more food than one person could eat,' indicating an increase in quantity.
  • 割豚胁 - Means 'cutting pork ribs,' specifying the type of food.
  • 堆以蒸饼 - Means 'piled with steamed buns,' describing the food arrangement.
  • 尽数人之餐 - Means 'eating as much as several people,' emphasizing the large amount consumed.
  • 果腹而谢 - Means 'full and thanked,' indicating the person's gratitude after eating.
  • 三年以来未尝如此饫饱 - Means 'not this well-fed for three years,' expressing the rarity of such a meal.
  • 固壮士 - Means 'indeed a warrior,' describing the person's strength.
  • 何飘泊若此 - Means 'why are you wandering like this,' questioning the person's situation.
  • 罪婴天谴 - Means 'punished by heaven,' indicating a sense of divine retribution.
  • 陆无屋，水无舟，朝村而暮郭也 - Means 'no house on land, no boat on water, living from village to town,' describing a nomadic lifestyle.
  • 整装欲行 - Means 'packing to leave,' indicating the narrator's intention to depart.
  • 其人相从，恋恋不去 - Means 'the person followed, reluctant to leave,' showing the person's attachment.
  • 君有大难，吾不忍忘一饭之德 - Means 'you will face great danger, I cannot forget the kindness of a single meal,' expressing concern and gratitude.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金陵 - Refers to Nanjing, a city with significant historical and cultural importance in China.
  • 旅舍 - Refers to an inn or lodging place, common in travel narratives.
  • 壮士 - Refers to a strong and brave person, often associated with warriors or heroes.
  • 天谴 - Refers to divine punishment, a concept often found in religious and moral teachings.
  • 朝村而暮郭 - Describes a nomadic lifestyle, moving between villages and towns, reflecting a transient existence.

Proper Nouns

  • 金陵 - The name of the city, Nanjing.
  • 旅舍 - The name of the inn, a generic term.

[406]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 樂異之 - 樂 means 'to be delighted,' and 異之 means 'to find it strange' or 'to regard it as unusual.' Together, it means 'the man found it strange and was delighted.'
  • 遂與偕行 - 遂 means 'thus' or 'therefore,' 與 means 'with,' and 偕行 means 'to travel together.' The phrase means 'he then traveled with him.'
  • 曳與同餐 - 曳 means 'to pull' or 'to drag,' and 同餐 means 'to eat together.' The phrase means 'he dragged him along to eat.'
  • 我終歲僅數餐耳 - 終歲 means 'throughout the year,' 僅 means 'only,' 數餐 means 'a few meals,' and 耳 is a particle indicating limitation. The phrase means 'I only have a few meals throughout the year.'
  • 益奇之 - 益 means 'more' or 'increasingly,' and 奇之 means 'to find it strange.' The phrase means 'he found it even stranger.'
  • 次日渡江 - 次日 means 'the next day,' and 渡江 means 'to cross the river.' The phrase means 'the next day, they crossed the river.'
  • 風涛暴作 - 风涛 means 'wind and waves,' and 暴作 means 'suddenly arise.' The phrase means 'the wind and waves suddenly arose.'
  • 估舟尽覆 - 估舟 means 'merchant ships,' and 尽覆 means 'all capsized.' The phrase means 'all the merchant ships capsized.'
  • 樂与其人悉没江中 - 悉 means 'all,' and 没江中 means 'drowned in the river.' The phrase means 'the man and the others all drowned in the river.'
  • 俄风定 - 俄 means 'soon,' and 风定 means 'the wind stopped.' The phrase means 'soon the wind stopped.'
  • 其人负乐踏波出 - 负 means 'to carry,' 踏波 means 'to tread on the waves,' and 出 means 'to emerge.' The phrase means 'the man carried him out by treading on the waves.'
  • 登客舟 - 登 means 'to board,' and 客舟 means 'the passenger boat.' The phrase means 'he boarded the passenger boat.'
  • 又破浪去 - 破浪 means 'to break through the waves,' and 去 means 'to go.' The phrase means 'and then went away by breaking through the waves.'
  • 少时挽一舟至 - 少时 means 'a short while later,' 挽 means 'to pull,' and 至 means 'to arrive.' The phrase means 'a short while later, he pulled a boat to them.'
  • 扶乐入 - 扶 means 'to support,' and 入 means 'to enter.' The phrase means 'he supported him into the boat.'
  • 嘱乐卧守 - 嘱 means 'to instruct,' 卧 means 'to lie down,' and 守 means 'to guard.' The phrase means 'he instructed him to lie down and guard.'
  • 复跃入江 - 复 means 'again,' 跃 means 'to leap,' and 入江 means 'into the river.' The phrase means 'he then leaped back into the river.'
  • 以两臂夹货出 - 以 means 'with,' 两臂 means 'two arms,' 夹 means 'to hold,' and 货出 means 'goods out.' The phrase means 'he held the goods out with his two arms.'
  • 掷舟中 - 掷 means 'to throw,' and 舟中 means 'into the boat.' The phrase means 'and threw them into the boat.'
  • 又入之 - 又 means 'again,' and 入之 means 'to enter it.' The phrase means 'and then entered it again.'
  • 数入数出 - 数 means 'several times,' and 入出 means 'to enter and exit.' The phrase means 'he entered and exited several times.'
  • 列货满舟 - 列 means 'to arrange,' 货 means 'goods,' and 满舟 means 'filling the boat.' The phrase means 'the goods were arranged and filled the boat.'
  • 乐谢曰 - 谢 means 'to thank,' and 曰 means 'said.' The phrase means 'the man thanked him and said.'
  • 君生我亦良足矣 - 君 means 'you,' 生我 means 'saved me,' 亦 means 'also,' 良 means 'very,' and 足矣 means 'sufficient.' The phrase means 'you saved me, which is very sufficient.'
  • 敢望珠还哉 - 敢 means 'dare,' 望 means 'hope,' 珠还 means 'the pearls return,' and 哉 is an exclamation particle. The phrase means 'how dare I hope for the pearls to return?'
  • 检视货财 - 检视 means 'to inspect,' and 货财 means 'goods and wealth.' The phrase means 'he inspected the goods and wealth.'
  • 并无亡失 - 并无 means 'not any,' and 亡失 means 'lost.' The phrase means 'and found no loss.'
  • 益喜 - 益 means 'more,' and 喜 means 'happy.' The phrase means 'he became even happier.'
  • 惊为神人 - 惊 means 'amazed,' 为 means 'to be,' and 神人 means 'a divine person.' The phrase means 'he was amazed to think him a divine person.'
  • 放舟欲行 - 放 means 'to set sail,' 舟 means 'boat,' and 欲行 means 'to want to go.' The phrase means 'he set the boat to sail and wanted to go.'
  • 其人告退 - 告 means 'to inform,' and 退 means 'to withdraw.' The phrase means 'the man informed him of his intention to withdraw.'
  • 乐苦留之 - 苦 means 'strongly,' 留 means 'to keep,' and 之 means 'him.' The phrase means 'the man strongly kept him.'
  • 遂与共济 - 遂 means 'thus,' 与 means 'with,' 共 means 'together,' and 济 means 'to cross.' The phrase means 'he then crossed with him.'
  • 乐笑云 - 笑 means 'to laugh,' and 云 means 'said.' The phrase means 'the man laughed and said.'
  • 此一厄也 - 此 means 'this,' 一 means 'one,' 厄 means 'calamity,' and 也 is a particle indicating a statement. The phrase means 'this was one calamity.'
  • 止失一金簪耳 - 止 means 'only,' 失 means 'lost,' 一 means 'one,' 金簪 means 'gold hairpin,' and 耳 is a particle indicating limitation. The phrase means 'only one gold hairpin was lost.'
  • 其人欲复寻之 - 欲 means 'wanted,' 复 means 'again,' 寻 means 'to search,' and 之 means 'it.' The phrase means 'the man wanted to search for it again.'
  • 乐方劝止 - 方 means 'just,' 劝 means 'to persuade,' and 止 means 'to stop.' The phrase means 'the man just persuaded him to stop.'
  • 已投水中而没 - 已 means 'already,' 投 means 'to throw,' 水中 means 'into the water,' and 没 means 'drowned.' The phrase means 'he had already thrown himself into the water and drowned.'

[407]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 驚愕良久 - Stunned and amazed for a long time; describes a prolonged state of shock and wonder.
  • 忽見含笑而出 - Suddenly saw smiling and coming out; describes an unexpected and cheerful appearance.
  • 以簪授樂曰 - Gave the hairpin to Le and said; indicates a direct speech act.
  • 幸不辱命 - Fortunately did not fail the mission; expresses gratitude and success in completing a task.
  • 罔不骇异 - No one was not shocked and amazed; emphasizes the widespread reaction of surprise.
  • 樂與歸 - Le accompanied him back; indicates a joint action.
  • 寢處共之 - Slept and lived together with him; describes a close and intimate relationship.
  • 每十數日始一食 - Ate only once every ten or so days; indicates an irregular eating pattern.
  • 食則啖嚼無算 - When eating, they chewed without counting; suggests a lack of restraint or measurement.
  • 又言別 - Said goodbye again; indicates a repeated farewell.
  • 樂固挽之 - Le firmly held him back; shows determination to prevent departure.
  • 適晝晦欲雨 - Just as the sky darkened and it seemed about to rain; sets a specific atmospheric condition.
  • 聞雷聲 - Heard the sound of thunder; adds an auditory element to the scene.
  • 雲間不知何狀 - I don't know what the shape of the clouds is; expresses ignorance about the appearance of the clouds.
  • 雷又是何物 - What is thunder?; asks a question about the nature of thunder.
  • 安得至天上視之 - How can I go to heaven to see it?; expresses a desire to observe something from a higher vantage point.
  • 君欲作雲中游耶 - Do you want to take a cloud tour?; responds to the desire with a question.
  • 伏榻假寐 - Lay down on the bed and took a nap; describes a brief rest.
  • 既醒 - After waking up; indicates the end of the nap.
  • 覺身搖搖然不似榻上 - Felt his body swaying as if not on the bed; describes a sensation of movement.
  • 開目則在雲氣中 - Opened his eyes and found himself in the cloud mist; indicates a sudden change in location.
  • 周身如絮 - Felt his whole body as if covered in cotton; describes a soft and fluffy sensation.
  • 踏之軟無地 - Stepped on it and it felt soft and like there was no ground; describes the sensation of walking on clouds.
  • 仰視星斗，在眉目間 - Looked up and saw the stars between his eyebrows; indicates a close proximity to the stars.
  • 遂疑是夢 - Then doubted if it was a dream; expresses uncertainty about the reality of the experience.
  • 細視星嵌天上如蓮實之在蓬也 - Looked closely and saw the stars embedded in the sky like lotus seeds in a lotus pod; describes the appearance of the stars.
  • 以手撼之 - Tried to shake it with his hand; indicates a physical interaction with the stars.
  • 大者堅不可動 - The larger ones were firm and could not be moved; describes the stability of the larger stars.
  • 小星搖動似可摘而下者 - The smaller stars were shaking and seemed to be able to be picked down; describes the movement of the smaller stars.
  • 遂摘其一藏袖中 - Then picked one and put it in his sleeve; indicates a specific action taken during the experience.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 驚愕 - A common reaction to unexpected or extraordinary events; reflects the emotional impact of the encounter.
  • 含笑而出 - A polite and friendly gesture; indicates a positive and welcoming demeanor.
  • 簪 - A hairpin, a traditional accessory often used by women; symbolizes elegance and refinement.
  • 樂 - The name of the protagonist; suggests a character who is active and engaged.
  • 罔不骇异 - A phrase that emphasizes the universal reaction of shock and amazement; reflects the extraordinary nature of the event.
  • 雲中游 - A metaphorical expression for a journey through the clouds; often associated with mystical or supernatural experiences.
  • 星斗 - Refers to the stars; often used in literature to symbolize guidance, destiny, or the unknown.

Proper Nouns

  • 樂 - The name of the protagonist; suggests a character who is active and engaged.
  • 江上人 - Refers to people on the riverbank; indicates the location of the event.
  • 雲中游 - A metaphorical expression for a journey through the clouds; often associated with mystical or supernatural experiences.
  • 星斗 - Refers to the stars; often used in literature to symbolize guidance, destiny, or the unknown.

[408]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 撥雲下視 - Literal phrase meaning 'to clear the clouds and look down,' used to describe seeing through or beyond something.
  • 銀河蒼茫 - Describes the Milky Way as vast and indistinct, evoking a sense of vastness and mystery.
  • 城郭如豆 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'cities like beans,' used to describe the small size of cities from a high vantage point.
  • 愕然自念 - Means 'startled and thinking to oneself,' indicating a moment of surprise and introspection.
  • 設一脫足，此身何可復向 - Rhetorical question meaning 'if one were to slip, how could this body return?' expressing a sense of danger and uncertainty.
  • 二龍夭矫 - Describes dragons as twisting and writhing, often used to depict powerful and agile creatures.
  • 駕缦車來 - Means 'to drive a carriage with a curtain,' describing a vehicle with a covering.
  • 尾一掉，如鳴牛鞭 - Vivid description of the dragon's tail moving like a sounding bullwhip, emphasizing the force and sound of the movement.
  • 以器掬水，遍洒云间 - Means 'to scoop water with a vessel and sprinkle it among the clouds,' describing a ritual or action involving water.
  • 忽見樂，共怪之 - Means 'suddenly seeing the joy, they all found it strange,' indicating a reaction to an unexpected event.
  • 樂審所與壯士在焉 - Means 'the joy recognized that the strong man was present,' indicating a realization or acknowledgment.
  • 因取一器授樂令洒 - Means 'therefore, taking a vessel and giving it to the joy to sprinkle,' describing an action of delegation.
  • 樂接器排云，遙望故乡，盡情傾注 - Means 'the joy took the vessel and pushed through the clouds, looking far away at the homeland, pouring out fully,' describing a vivid and emotional action.
  • 未幾謂樂曰 - Means 'not long after, he said to the joy,' indicating a passage of time and a conversation.
  • 我本雷曹，前誤行雨，罰谪三载 - Means 'I was originally the Thunder Officer, but made a mistake in the rain, and was punished with exile for three years,' indicating a backstory and a reason for the current situation.
  • 今天限已滿，请从此别 - Means 'now the time limit is over, please part ways here,' indicating the end of a period and a request to leave.
  • 乃以驾车之绳万丈掷前，使握端缒下 - Means 'he then threw a rope ten thousand feet long in front, and had him hold the end and descend,' describing a method of descent.
  • 樂危之 - Means 'the joy was cautious about it,' indicating a sense of caution or concern.
  • 其人笑言 - Means 'the person laughed and said,' indicating a response with humor.
  • 不妨 - Means 'it's not a problem,' indicating reassurance.
  • 樂如其言，飗飗然瞬息及地 - Means 'the joy did as he said, and in a moment, swiftly reached the ground,' describing a quick and smooth descent.
  • 視之，則堕立村外 - Means 'looking at it, it was standing outside the village,' indicating the final location.
  • 绳渐收入云中，不可见矣 - Means 'the rope gradually retracted into the clouds, becoming invisible,' indicating the end of the action.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 雷曹 - Refers to a mythical figure or deity associated with thunder and rain, often seen in Chinese mythology.
  • 行雨 - Refers to the act of causing rain, often associated with deities or supernatural beings.
  • 谪 - Refers to a form of punishment or exile, often used in the context of divine retribution.
  • 驾车之绳 - Refers to a rope used for descending, often associated with mythical or supernatural contexts.
  • 村外 - Refers to the outskirts of a village, indicating a rural setting.

Proper Nouns

  • 雷曹 - The mythical figure or deity associated with thunder and rain.
  • 驾车之绳 - The rope used for descending.
  • 村外 - The outskirts of the village.

[409]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 时久旱 - Means 'for a long time there was a drought.'
  • 十里外雨仅盈指 - Literally 'rain outside ten li only fills a finger,' meaning it rained lightly outside the village.
  • 独乐里沟浍皆满 - Means 'only in Le Village, the ditches and canals were all full.'
  • 归探袖中 - Means 'returning and searching in the sleeve.'
  • 摘星仍在 - Means 'the star-picking tool was still there.'
  • 出置案上 - Means 'taking it out and placing it on the table.'
  • 黯黝如石 - Means 'dark and black like stone.'
  • 入夜则光明焕发 - Means 'at night it shines brightly.'
  • 映照四壁 - Means 'illuminating all four walls.'
  • 益宝之 - Means 'treasuring it even more.'
  • 什袭而藏 - Means 'treasuring it and hiding it carefully.'
  • 每有佳客 - Means 'whenever there are good guests.'

  • 窗间零帛断绵，指印宛然 - '零帛断绵' (líng bó duàn mián) refers to torn cloth and broken silk, and '指印宛然' (zhǐ yìn wǎn rán) means 'fingerprints clearly visible,' suggesting that the items had been handled.
  • 惟舍后遗一小白石缸 - '惟舍后' (wéi shě hòu) means 'only behind the house,' and '遗一小白石缸' (yí yī xiǎo bái shí gāng) means 'left a small white stone basin,' indicating a specific object left behind.
  • 可受石许 - '可受' (kě shòu) means 'can hold,' and '石许' (shí xǔ) means 'about a stone's worth,' indicating the size of the basin.
  • 尹携归贮水养朱鱼 - '携归' (xié guī) means 'took back,' and '贮水养朱鱼' (zhù shuǐ yǎng zhū yú) means 'stored water to keep red fish,' indicating that the person (尹) used the basin to keep fish.
  • 经年水清如初贮 - '经年' (jīng nián) means 'for many years,' and '水清如初贮' (shuǐ qīng rú chū zhù) means 'the water remained as clear as when it was first stored,' indicating that the water stayed clear over time.
  • 后为佣保移石误碎之 - '后为佣保' (hòu wéi yōng bǎo) means 'later by a servant,' and '移石误碎之' (yí shí wù suì zhī) means 'moved the stone and accidentally broke it,' indicating an accidental event.
  • 水蓄并不倾泻 - '水蓄' (shuǐ xù) means 'water stored,' and '并不倾泻' (bìng bù qīng xiè) means 'did not spill out,' indicating that the water remained contained.
  • 视之缸宛在，扪之虚软 - '视之' (shì zhī) means 'when looked at,' and '扪之虚软' (mén zhī xū ruǎn) means 'when touched, felt soft and empty,' indicating that the basin appeared to be there but was actually hollow.
  • 手入其中，水随手泄，出其手则复合 - '手入其中' (shǒu rù qí zhōng) means 'when the hand was inserted,' and '水随手泄' (shuǐ suí shǒu xiè) means 'the water flowed out,' and '出其手则复合' (chū qí shǒu zé hé fù) means 'when the hand was withdrawn, it closed again,' indicating a magical or supernatural property.
  • 冬月不冰 - '冬月' (dōng yuè) means 'winter month,' and '不冰' (bù bīng) means 'does not freeze,' indicating that the water did not freeze in winter.
  • 一夜忽结为晶 - '一夜忽结' (yī yè hū jié) means 'one night suddenly froze,' and '为晶' (wéi jīng) means 'into crystal,' indicating that the water froze into a crystal form.
  • 鱼游如故 - '鱼游' (yú yóu) means 'fish swimming,' and '如故' (rú gù) means 'as before,' indicating that the fish continued to swim as usual.
  • 尹畏人知，常置密室，非子婿不以示也 - '尹畏人知' (yǐn wèi rén zhī) means '尹 was afraid people would know,' and '常置密室' (cháng zhì mì shì) means 'often placed in a secret room,' and '非子婿不以示也' (fēi zǐ xù bù yǐ shì yě) means 'only showed to his son-in-law,' indicating that the person (尹) kept the basin hidden.
  • 久之渐播，索玩者纷错于门 - '久之渐播' (jiǔ zhī jiàn bō) means 'after a long time, it gradually spread,' and '索玩者纷错于门' (suǒ wán zhě fēn cuò yú mén) means 'people seeking to play with it crowded at the door,' indicating that the news of the basin spread and attracted many visitors.
  • 腊月忽解为水 - '腊月' (là yuè) means 'the twelfth month of the lunar calendar,' and '忽解为水' (hū jiě wéi shuǐ) means 'suddenly melted into water,' indicating that the crystal form of the water melted back into liquid.
  • 阴湿满地，鱼亦渺然 - '阴湿满地' (yīn shī mǎn dì) means 'dampness filled the ground,' and '鱼亦渺然' (yú yì miǎo rán) means 'the fish also disappeared,' indicating that the fish were no longer visible.
  • 其旧缸残石犹存 - '其旧缸' (qí jiù gāng) means 'the old basin,' and '残石犹存' (cán shí yóu cún) means 'the broken stone remains,' indicating that the broken pieces of the basin were still there.

  • 嫁我犹不嫁也 - The frustration of unfulfilled desires was a common theme.
  • 是非我之能为力矣 - Recognizing one's limitations was a realistic and humble approach.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 我曰嫁且恐不得当 - Colloquial expression where '我曰' means 'I said,' '嫁' means 'to marry,' '且恐' means 'and fear,' and '不得当' means 'not to be suitable' or 'not to be appropriate.' Translated as 'I said that marrying would be fearful and not appropriate.'
  • 必无取直焉 - Formal expression where '必' means 'certainly,' '无' means 'not,' '取' means 'to take' or 'to accept,' and '直' means 'straight' or 'honest.' Translated as 'certainly not to accept the straightforward path.'
  • 是大人所必不允 - Formal expression where '是' means 'this,' '大人' means 'the elder' or 'the superior,' '所' is a marker for the subject of the following verb, '必' means 'certainly,' and '不允' means 'not to permit.' Translated as 'this is certainly something that the elder will not permit.'
  • 亦余所不敢言也 - Formal expression where '亦' means 'also,' '余' means 'I,' '所' is a marker for the subject of the following verb, '不敢' means 'not dare,' and '言' means 'to speak.' Translated as 'also something that I dare not speak.'
  • 梅闻之泣下 - Colloquial expression where '梅闻之' means 'Mei heard it,' and '泣下' means 'to weep.' Translated as 'Mei heard it and wept.'
  • 但求怜拯 - Colloquial expression where '但' means 'only,' '求' means 'to seek,' '怜' means 'mercy,' and '拯' means 'to rescue.' Translated as 'only seeking mercy and rescue.'
  • 女思良久 - Colloquial expression where '女' means 'the woman,' '思' means 'to think,' and '良久' means 'for a long time.' Translated as 'the woman thought for a long time.'
  • 无已 - Colloquial expression meaning 'no way' or 'no choice.'
  • 我私蓄数金 - Formal expression where '我' means 'I,' '私蓄' means 'private savings,' and '数金' means 'some money.' Translated as 'I have some private savings.'
  • 倾囊相助 - Colloquial expression where '倾囊' means 'to empty the bag' (i.e., to give all one has), and '相助' means 'to help.' Translated as 'to help by giving all I have.'
  • 藏待好音 - Colloquial expression where '藏' means 'to hide' or 'to store,' '待' means 'to wait,' and '好音' means 'good news.' Translated as 'to store and wait for good news.'
  • 会王授曲沃宰 - Formal expression where '会' means 'just then' or 'at that time,' '王' means 'the king,' '授' means 'to appoint,' and '曲沃宰' means 'the governor of Quwo.' Translated as 'just then the king appointed Wang as the governor of Quwo.'
  • 喜乘间告母曰 - Colloquial expression where '喜' means 'happy,' '乘间' means 'taking the opportunity,' '告' means 'to tell,' '母' means 'mother,' and '曰' means 'said.' Translated as 'happy, taking the opportunity to tell his mother, said.'
  • 青梅年已长 - Formal expression where '青梅' means 'Qingmei,' '年' means 'age,' '已' means 'already,' and '长' means 'grown.' Translated as 'Qingmei has already grown up.'
  • 今将莅任 - Formal expression where '今' means 'now,' '将' means 'about to,' '莅' means 'to assume office,' and '任' means 'duty' or 'position.' Translated as 'now about to assume office.'
  • 不如遣之 - Colloquial expression where '不如' means 'had better not,' '遣' means 'to send away,' and '之' means 'her.' Translated as 'had better not send her away.'
  • 夫人固以青梅太黠 - Formal expression where '夫人' means 'the lady' or 'the wife,' '固' means 'certainly,' '以' means 'because,' '青梅' means 'Qingmei,' '太' means 'too,' and '黠' means 'clever.' Translated as 'the lady certainly thought Qingmei was too clever.'
  • 恐导女不义 - Formal expression where '恐' means 'fear,' '导' means 'to guide,' '女' means 'her,' and '不义' means 'unjust.' Translated as 'fearing that guiding her would be unjust.'
  • 每欲嫁之 - Colloquial expression where '每' means 'often,' '欲' means 'to want,' '嫁' means 'to marry,' and '之' means 'her.' Translated as 'often wanted to marry her.'
  • 而恐女不乐也 - Colloquial expression where '而' means 'but,' '恐' means 'fear,' '女' means 'she,' '不' means 'not,' and '乐' means 'happy.' Translated as 'but feared that she would not be happy.'
  • 闻女言甚喜 - Colloquial expression where '闻' means 'heard,' '女' means 'her,' '言' means 'words,' and '甚喜' means 'very happy.' Translated as 'heard her words and was very happy.'
  • 逾两日 - Colloquial expression where '逾' means 'after,' '两' means 'two,' and '日' means 'days.' Translated as 'after two days.'
  • 有佣保妇白张氏意 - Formal expression where '有' means 'there was,' '佣保妇' means 'a servant woman,' '白' means 'to inform,' '张氏' means 'Zhang's,' and '意' means 'intention.' Translated as 'there was a servant woman who informed Zhang's intention.'
  • 是只合偶婢子 - Formal expression where '是' means 'this,' '只' means 'only,' '合' means 'fit,' '偶' means 'to match,' and '婢子' means 'maid.' Translated as 'this only fits to match a maid.'
  • 前此何妄也 - Colloquial expression where '前此' means 'before this,' '何' means 'how,' '妄' means 'foolish,' and '也' is a sentence-ending particle. Translated as 'how foolish before this!'
  • 然鬻媵高门 - Formal expression where '然' means 'but,' '鬻' means 'to sell,' '媵' means 'concubine,' and '高门' means 'high-ranking family.' Translated as 'but selling a concubine to a high-ranking family.'
  • 价当倍于曩昔 - Formal expression where '价' means 'price,' '当' means 'should,' '倍' means 'double,' '于' means 'than,' and '曩昔' means 'before.' Translated as 'the price should double that before.'
  • 卖为妾 - Colloquial expression where '卖' means 'to sell,' '为' means 'as,' and '妾' means 'concubine.' Translated as 'to sell as a concubine.'
  • 良不忍 - Colloquial expression where '良' means 'indeed,' '不忍' means 'cannot bear.' Translated as 'indeed cannot bear.'
  • 以青梅嫔于生 - Formal expression where '以' means 'to make,' '青梅' means 'Qingmei,' '嫔' means 'to become a concubine,' and '于' means 'to,' '生' means 'him.' Translated as 'to make Qingmei become a concubine to him.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嫁且恐不得当 - Reflects the social norms and expectations surrounding marriage in classical Chinese society, where suitability and appropriateness were crucial considerations.
  • 必无取直焉 - Suggests a rejection of straightforward or direct approaches, which could be culturally significant in contexts where indirectness and subtlety are valued.
  • 是大人所必不允 - Refers to a respected or authoritative figure, reflecting the hierarchical nature of classical Chinese society.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王如晋半载 - Literally 'the king was like Jin for half a year,' likely referring to a period of time or a state of being similar to the Jin dynasty. The exact meaning depends on context.
  • 夫人卒 - 'The lady died,' where '夫人' refers to the king's wife or a high-ranking lady.
  • 停柩寺中 - 'The coffin was left in the temple,' where '柩' means coffin and '寺' refers to a temple or monastery.
  • 坐行赇免 - 'He was dismissed for accepting bribes,' where '坐' means 'for the reason of' or 'because of,' and '行赇' means 'accepting bribes.'
  • 罚赎万计 - 'He was fined tens of thousands of times,' where '罚赎' means 'fines and redemption payments,' and '万计' means 'in the tens of thousands.'
  • 从者逃散 - 'His followers fled and dispersed,' where '从者' means 'followers' or 'attendants.'
  • 是时疫大作 - 'At that time, a great epidemic broke out,' where '疫大作' means 'a major epidemic' or 'an outbreak of plague.'
  • 染疾卒 - 'He contracted an illness and died,' where '染疾' means 'contracted an illness,' and '卒' means 'died.'
  • 惟一媪从女 - 'Only one old woman accompanied the girl,' where '媪' means 'old woman' or 'matron.'
  • 未几媪亦卒 - 'Soon after, the old woman also died,' where '未几' means 'soon after' or 'not long after.'
  • 女伶仃益苦 - 'The girl became increasingly lonely and miserable,' where '伶仃' means 'lonely' or 'miserable,' and '益苦' means 'increasingly miserable.'
  • 有邻媪劝之嫁 - 'An elderly neighbor woman advised her to marry,' where '邻媪' means 'elderly neighbor woman,' and '劝之嫁' means 'advised her to marry.'
  • 能为我双葬亲者，从之 - 'If you can bury my parents together, I will follow you,' where '双葬亲' means 'bury my parents together,' and '从之' means 'follow you' or 'agree to marry you.'
  • 日仅一餐，延息待贾 - 'She ate only one meal a day, barely surviving while waiting for a husband,' where '延息' means 'barely surviving,' and '待贾' means 'waiting for a husband.'
  • 困顿如此，每欲自尽 - 'In such a state of distress, she often wanted to commit suicide,' where '困顿' means 'in a state of distress,' and '每欲自尽' means 'often wanted to commit suicide.'
  • 犹恋恋而苟活者，徒以有两柩在 - 'She still clung to life out of mere attachment, because she had two coffins,' where '恋恋而苟活' means 'clung to life out of mere attachment,' and '两柩' means 'two coffins.'
  • 故思不如依汝言也 - 'Therefore, I think it's better to follow your advice,' where '故思' means 'therefore think,' and '依汝言' means 'follow your advice.'
  • 微窥女，大悦 - 'He secretly looked at the girl and was greatly pleased,' where '微窥' means 'secretly looked,' and '大悦' means 'greatly pleased.'
  • 即出金营葬，双槥具举 - 'He immediately paid in gold for the burial, and both coffins were buried,' where '营葬' means 'arranged for the burial,' and '双槥具举' means 'both coffins were buried.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王如晋半载 - The reference to 'Jin' likely alludes to the Jin dynasty, a period of Chinese history known for its political and cultural significance.
  • 停柩寺中 - The practice of leaving a coffin in a temple reflects the cultural and religious customs of the time, where temples were often used for funerals and memorial services.
  • 坐行赇免 - The mention of accepting bribes and being dismissed reflects the political corruption and legal practices of the period.
  • 罚赎万计 - The heavy fines and redemption payments indicate the severity of the punishment for corruption and the financial burden it placed on the individual.
  • 从者逃散 - The flight and dispersal of followers highlight the social and political instability that often accompanied the downfall of a ruler.
  • 是时疫大作 - The reference to a major epidemic reflects the frequent occurrence of plagues and diseases in Chinese history and their impact on society.
  • 染疾卒 - The death from illness underscores the vulnerability of individuals to disease and the high mortality rates of the time.
  • 惟一媪从女 - The presence of an old woman as the only companion highlights the isolation and lack of support for the girl.
  • 未几媪亦卒 - The death of the old woman emphasizes the girl's increasing loneliness and vulnerability.
  • 女伶仃益苦 - The girl's state of loneliness and misery reflects the harsh realities faced by women in traditional Chinese society.
  • 有邻媪劝之嫁 - The advice to marry from a neighbor woman reflects the social norms and expectations placed on women to find a husband.
  • 能为我双葬亲者，从之 - The condition of burying her parents together before agreeing to marry reflects the cultural importance of filial piety and the desire to honor one's parents.
  • 日仅一餐，延息待贾 - The girl's struggle to survive on minimal food and her hope for marriage reflect the economic hardships and social pressures faced by women in the period.
  • 困顿如此，每欲自尽 - The girl's contemplation of suicide highlights the extreme emotional and psychological distress she experiences.
  • 犹恋恋而苟活者，徒以有两柩在 - The girl's attachment to life out of mere attachment to her parents' coffins reflects the deep cultural and emotional significance of filial piety.
  • 故思不如依汝言也 - The girl's decision to follow the advice of the elderly neighbor woman reflects the influence of social norms and the desire for a better future.
  • 微窥女，大悦 - The man's secret admiration of the girl and his subsequent actions reflect the social dynamics and power imbalances in the period.
  • 即出金营葬，双槥具举 - The immediate payment of gold for the burial and the burial of both coffins reflect the cultural and social practices surrounding death and burial.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '自浮海去，家人无不谓其已死' is elliptical, but it means 'since he set sail across the sea, everyone in the family thought he was dead.'
  • The sentence '及至家人皆诧异' is elliptical, but it means 'when he finally arrived home, everyone was astonished.'
  • The sentence '幸翁媪无恙，独妻已去帷' is elliptical, but it means 'fortunately, the elderly couple were unharmed, but only the wife had left the curtain.'
  • The sentence '乃悟龙女‘守义’之言，盖已先知也' is elliptical, but it means 'he then understood the dragon maiden's words of 'loyalty,' it was already foreseen.'
  • The sentence '父欲为生再婚，生不可，纳婢焉' is elliptical, but it means 'the father wanted to arrange another marriage for him, he refused, and instead took a maid as his concubine.'
  • The sentence '谨志三年之期，泛舟岛中' is elliptical, but it means 'he carefully noted the three-year period, and sailed to the island.'

'切望' means 'earnestly hoped,' '姑一临存' means 'come to visit once,' and '终以负债为憾' means 'was always reluctant to come because of his debt.'

  • 抑何其神 - Exclamatory phrase meaning 'how extraordinary' or 'how remarkable.'
  • 使荊卿能爾，則千載無遺恨矣 - Hypothetical statement suggesting that if Jing Ke had acted in this way, there would have been no lasting regret for a thousand years; can be translated as 'If Jing Ke had been able to do this, there would have been no lasting regret for a thousand years.'
  • 苟有其人，可以補天網之漏 - Metaphor for someone who can correct or improve a flawed system; can be translated as 'If such a person exists, they could fill the gaps in the heavenly net.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 阿爹故家子，今亦‘穷波斯’，落落不称意 - This sentence describes the father's current state of being from a noble background but now in poverty, and feeling discontented.
  • 旦晚与儿还往 - This phrase suggests a close relationship and frequent interaction.
  • 笑弯秋月，羞晕朝霞，实天人也 - This poetic description emphasizes the beauty of the person being described.
  • 可知是大家，蜗庐人焉得如此娟好 - This rhetorical question contrasts the noble background with the humble one, emphasizing the beauty of the person.
  • 女学士，诗词俱大高作 - This phrase describes the woman's literary and intellectual achievements.
  • 小婢无端败坏人，教阿舅齿冷也 - This playful remark suggests that the maid's words are embarrassing.
  • 舅断弦未续，若个小娘子，颇能快意否 - This question asks if the uncle would be happy if he were to marry the young woman.
  • 婢子颠疯作也 - This playful remark suggests that the maid is acting crazy.
  • 言虽近戏，而生殊爱好之 - This phrase indicates that although the words are playful, the young man really likes her.
  • 舅倘不以粪壤致猜，儿当请诸其母 - This polite request asks if the uncle would not object, and if so, the speaker will ask the mother for permission.

  • 径造庐访成 - He went straight to Cheng's house to visit him, indicating a direct and possibly urgent visit.
  • 视成所蓄，掩口胡卢而笑 - Seeing what Cheng had, he laughed heartily, describing the youth's reaction to Cheng's collection of insects.
  • 因出己虫，纳比笼中 - He then took out his own insect and put it in the same cage, setting up a comparison or competition.
  • 成视之，庞然修伟，自增惭怍，不敢与较 - Cheng looked at it, seeing how large and impressive it was, feeling ashamed and unwilling to compete, expressing Cheng's feelings of inadequacy.
  • 少年固强之 - The youth insisted on it, showing the youth's persistence.
  • 顾念：蓄劣物终无所用，不如拚博一笑 - But Cheng thought: keeping inferior things is useless; it's better to have a laugh, reflecting Cheng's pragmatic attitude.
  • 因合纳斗盆 - So he put them together in the fighting bowl, preparing for the match.
  • 小虫伏不动，蠢若木鸡 - The small insect lay still, appearing as dull as a wooden chicken, describing the insect's state before the fight.
  • 屡撩之，虫暴怒，直奔，遂相腾击，振奋作声 - After being provoked several times, the insect suddenly became furious, charging forward, and they began to leap and fight, making a commotion, detailing the progression of the battle.
  • 俄见小虫跃起，张尾伸须，直龁敌领 - Soon, the small insect leaped up, spreading its tail and antennae, biting the opponent directly, describing a specific action during the fight.
  • 少年大骇，解令休止 - The youth was greatly startled and ordered them to stop, showing the youth's reaction to the unexpected turn of events.
  • 虫翘然矜鸣，似报主知 - The insect proudly chirped, as if reporting to its master, indicating the insect's victory and Cheng's pride.
  • 成大喜 - Cheng was very pleased, concluding the story with Cheng's satisfaction.

  • 子弟 - Refers to young men or students, often used in the context of education or competition.
  • 斗盆 - A container used for insect fighting, a common pastime in traditional Chinese culture.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俄有金甲神人 - Soon there appeared a god clad in golden armor. *俄* means 'soon' or 'in a short while.' *金甲* is 'golden armor,' a common description of divine or celestial beings. *神人* is 'god' or 'divine person.'
  • 捧黄帛书至 - carrying a yellow silk scroll to him. *黄帛* is 'yellow silk,' often used in classical texts to describe sacred or imperial scrolls. *书* is 'scroll' or 'document.'
  • 群拜舞启读已 - the crowd bowed, danced, and opened it to read. *群拜舞* is a compound expression meaning 'the crowd bowed and danced in reverence.' *启读* means 'to open and read.' *已* is a particle indicating the completion of the action.
  • 君有回阳之机矣 - You have the opportunity to return to the world of the living. *回阳* is an idiom meaning 'to return to the living' or 'to revive from death.' *之机* is 'the opportunity' or 'the chance.'
  • 适接帝诏，大赦幽冥 - Just now I received the imperial decree, a great amnesty for the underworld. *适* means 'just now' or 'recently.' *帝诏* is 'the emperor's decree.' *大赦幽冥* is a formal expression meaning 'great amnesty for the underworld' or 'a general pardon for the dead.'
  • 可为君委折原例耳 - It can be applied to you as an exception. *委折原例* is a legal or bureaucratic term meaning 'to apply an exception' or 'to grant clemency.'
  • 数武之外 - a few steps away. *数武* is an idiom meaning 'a few steps' or 'a short distance.' *武* was an ancient unit of distance, roughly equivalent to a step or pace.
  • 冥黑如漆 - as dark as ink. *冥黑* is 'pitch-black' or 'extremely dark.' *如漆* is 'like ink,' a common simile in classical Chinese for total darkness.
  • 不辨行路 - could not tell the way to go. *辨* is 'to distinguish' or 'to discern.' *行路* is 'the way to go' or 'the path.'
  • 公甚窘苦 - the official was very distressed. *窘苦* is a compound word meaning 'distressed' or 'helpless.'
  • 轩然而入 - entered majestically. *轩然* is an adverb meaning 'majestically' or 'with dignity.'
  • 赤面长髯 - red-faced and long-bearded. *赤面* is 'red face,' often used to describe a fierce or wrathful appearance. *长髯* is 'long beard.'
  • 光射数尺 - his light shone several feet. *光射* is 'light radiated' or 'shone.' *数尺* is 'a few feet,' a measurement of distance.
  • 公迎拜而哀之 - the official bowed and wept in supplication. *迎拜* is 'to bow in greeting' or 'to bow in supplication.' *哀之* is 'to weep for him' or 'to mourn for him.'
  • 诵佛经可出 - reciting the Buddhist sutras will allow you to escape. *诵佛经* is 'to recite Buddhist scriptures.' *可出* is 'can escape' or 'can leave.'
  • 合掌而诵 - joined his palms and recited. *合掌* is a gesture of prayer or reverence, commonly associated with Buddhist practice.
  • 顿觉一线光明 - suddenly felt a single line of light. *顿觉* is 'suddenly realized' or 'suddenly felt.' *一线光明* is 'a single line of light' or 'a thin ray of light.'
  • 映照前路 - illuminated the path ahead. *映照* is 'to illuminate' or 'to shine upon.' *前路* is 'the path ahead.'
  • 偶有遗忘，则目前顿黑 - if he forgot, then immediately before his eyes it turned black. *偶有* is 'occasionally' or 'sometimes.' *遗忘* is 'to forget.' *目前顿黑* is 'before his eyes turned black' or 'the vision turned black.'
  • 定想移时 - he focused his thoughts for a while. *定想* is 'to concentrate one’s thoughts' or 'to fix one’s mind.' *移时* is 'for a while' or 'a short period of time.'
  • 复诵复明 - he recited again and the light returned. *复诵* is 'recited again.' *复明* is 'the light returned' or 'the brightness returned.'
  • 乃始得出 - then he was finally able to escape. *乃* is a conjunction meaning 'then' or 'therefore.' *始* is 'only then' or 'finally.' *得出* is 'to escape' or 'to exit.'
  • 其二役，则不可问矣 - as for the two attendants, it is not worth asking about. *其二役* is 'the two attendants' or 'the two attendants (who were left behind).' *则不可问矣* is 'then it is not worth inquiring about' or 'it is not worth mentioning.'

  • 汝师百金尚不能任，焉有福泽从我老尚书也 - Your teacher cannot even handle a hundred gold coins, how could she have any blessings to accompany my old minister? '任' means to handle or manage, and '焉有' means how could there be.
  • 遂以五十金付尼去 - So they gave her fifty gold coins and let her go. '付' means to give or hand over.

Describes Zeng's varying levels of hospitality.
  • 晋抚馈女乐十人，皆是好女子，其尤者为袅袅，为仙仙，二人尤蒙宠顾 - '晋抚馈女乐十人' means 'the governor of Shanxi presented ten female entertainers,' '皆是好女子' means 'all were beautiful women,' '其尤者为袅袅，为仙仙' means 'the best among them were named Niaoniao and Xianxian,' and '二人尤蒙宠顾' means 'who were especially favored by him.' Idiomatic Expressions

  • 異史氏曰 - A formal opening used by the author of *Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio* to introduce commentary or moral reflections.
  • 夢固為妄，想亦非真 - A philosophical statement contrasting dreams as illusions with imagination as not entirely real.
  • 彼以虛作，神以幻報 - A metaphorical statement suggesting that the gods respond to false creations with illusions.
  • 黃粱將熟，此夢在所必有 - A reference to the *Yellow Millet Dream*, a famous Chinese parable about the fleeting nature of life and dreams.
  • 當以附之邯鄲之後 - A historical allusion to the *Yellow Millet Dream*, which occurred in the city of Handan (邯鄲).
  • 徐東痴夜南游 - Describes a character named Xu Dongchi traveling south at night.
  • 泊舟江岸 - Literally 'mooring the boat by the riverbank,' indicating a temporary stop.
  • 見一蒼龍自空垂下 - A vivid description of a blue dragon descending from the sky.
  • 波浪涌起，隨龍身而上 - The waves rise and follow the dragon's body.
  • 遙望水光閃閃，闊於三尺練 - A poetic description of the water's shimmering appearance, comparing it to a wide silk ribbon.
  • 移時龍尾收去，水亦頓息 - After a while, the dragon's tail retracts, and the water calms down.
  • 俄而大雨傾注，渠道皆平 - Suddenly, heavy rain pours down, filling the ditches.

  • 異史氏 - The pseudonym of the author Pu Songling, who wrote *Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio*.
  • 邯鄲 - The city where the *Yellow Millet Dream* is said to have occurred, adding historical and cultural depth to the story.
  • 蒼龍 - A blue dragon, often associated with water and the element of water in Chinese mythology.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逡巡局侧，耽玩不去 - 逡巡 means 'to hesitate or linger,' 局侧 refers to the side of the chessboard, and 耽玩 means 'to be engrossed in play.' Together, they describe someone who is deeply absorbed in the game.
  • 目面寒俭，悬鹑结焉 - 目面寒俭 describes a person with a plain or unremarkable appearance, and 悬鹑结焉 uses the metaphor of a tattered robe to depict someone in poor condition.
  • 意态温雅，有文士风 - 意态温雅 means 'gentle and refined demeanor,' and 文士风 refers to the manner of a cultured scholar.
  • 分指棋谓曰 - 分指棋 means 'pointing to the chessboard,' and 谓曰 means 'to say to,' indicating a gesture of invitation.
  • 先生当必善此，何不与客对垒 - 善此 means 'to be skilled at this,' and 对垒 means 'to face off in a game.' The phrase is an invitation to play chess.
  • 逊谢移时 - 逊谢 means 'to decline politely,' and 移时 means 'for a long time,' indicating a prolonged period of hesitation.
  • 局终而负，神情懊热，若不自己 - 局终而负 means 'losing the game,' 情神懊热 describes a frustrated or angry expression, and 若不自己 means 'as if unable to control oneself.'
  • 又着又负，益愤惭 - 又着又负 means 'losing again after another move,' and 益愤惭 means 'becoming even more angry and ashamed.'
  • 酌之以酒，亦不饮 - 酌之以酒 means 'to offer wine,' and 亦不饮 means 'but he did not drink.'
  • 惟曳客弈 - 惟曳客弈 means 'only to pull the guest into another game,' indicating a focus on chess.
  • 自晨至于日昃，不遑溲溺 - 自晨至于日昃 means 'from morning until noon,' and 不遑溲溺 means 'without a moment to relieve oneself,' emphasizing the intensity of the game.
  • 方以一子争路，两互喋聒 - 方以一子争路 means 'struggling over one move,' and 两互喋聒 means 'arguing back and forth,' describing a heated dispute.

  • 時無晨炊，向公哀乞，公辄给以升斗 - '時無晨炊' means 'at times had no morning meal'; '向公哀乞' means 'appealed to Master Li in sorrowful request'; '公辄给以升斗' means 'Master Li would give him a measure of grain (升斗, a unit of measure).'

  • 然何可以久？乞主人贷我绿豆一石作资本 - '然何可以久' means 'but how can this continue?' or 'but how can this last?'; '乞主人贷我绿豆一石作资本' means 'I beg the master to lend me one dan of green beans as capital.'
  • 公忻然授之 - '忻然' means 'gladly' or 'happily'; '授之' means 'gave it to him.'
  • 卓负去，年余，一无所偿 - '负去' means 'took it away' or 'carried it off'; '年余' means 'more than a year'; '一无所偿' means 'returned nothing' or 'did not repay anything.'

  • 升斗 - A unit of measure for grain, commonly used in ancient China. One '升' is approximately 0.2 liters, and '斗' is ten '升,' so '升斗' is a small measure, likely used for daily sustenance.
  • 绿豆一石 - '绿豆' is green mung beans, a common legume used as food and for trade; '一石' is a larger unit of measure, equivalent to 10 '斗' or approximately 20 liters. This amount would be a significant capital for a poor worker.

  • 置不索 - A phrase indicating that the lender chose to forgive the debt and not pursue repayment, a gesture of mercy.

  • 寻途登觇 - This phrase suggests a methodical approach to exploring the source of the sound, which adds a layer of curiosity and investigation to the narrative.
  • 围如碗 - This phrase provides a vivid description of the snake's size, which is important for understanding the scale of the struggle.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 仆人怪似其主 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the servant is strangely similar to his master.' The word *怪* conveys a sense of striking or unusual resemblance.
  • 反复凝注之 - Means 'to gaze at repeatedly' or 'to stare intently,' suggesting the master’s fascination with the child.
  • 仆笑以对，王亦笑 - A simple but culturally significant expression of mutual amusement, common in classical narratives.
  • 细视儿，风度磊落 - 'Looking closely at the child, he had a noble and upright bearing.' *风度* refers to demeanor or bearing, and *磊落* suggests honesty or integrity.
  • 自念乏嗣 - 'He thought to himself that he lacked an heir.' *乏嗣* is a classical term for childlessness or lack of descendants.
  • 因其肖己，爱而赎之 - 'Because the child resembled himself, he loved him and bought him.' *肖己* means 'resembling oneself.'
  • 诘其名，自称王孜 - 'He asked for his name, and the child said his name was Wang Zi.' *诘* means to question or inquire.
  • 子弃之襁褓，何知姓氏？ - 'You were abandoned as an infant, how could you know your surname?' This is a rhetorical question reflecting the king’s surprise.
  • 得我时，胸前有字，书山东王文之子 - 'When I was found, there was writing on my chest, stating I was the son of Wang Wen of Shandong.' *书* here means 'to write' or 'to inscribe.'
  • 心窃喜，甚爱惜之 - 'He secretly rejoiced and cherished him greatly.' *心窃喜* is a classical way of expressing private joy.
  • 及归，见者不问而知为王生子 - 'When he returned, those who saw him knew without being asked that this was the king’s son.' *不问而知* implies obviousness.
  • 孜渐长，孔武有力 - 'As Zi grew older, he became strong and powerful.' *孔武* is a classical term for strength or martial prowess.
  • 喜田猎，不务生产 - 'He loved hunting and did not focus on productive work.' *不务生产* refers to not engaging in agricultural or economic labor.
  • 乐斗好杀 - 'He loved fighting and was fond of killing.' This is a stark characterization of the child’s violent tendencies.
  • 王亦不能钳制之 - 'The king could not control him.' *钳制* means to restrain or control.
  • 自言能见鬼狐 - 'He claimed he could see ghosts and fox spirits.' *鬼狐* refers to supernatural beings, particularly fox spirits in Chinese folklore.
  • 悉不之信 - 'He did not believe him at all.' *悉不之信* is a classical construction meaning 'completely did not believe.'
  • 会里中有患狐者，请孜往觇之 - 'When a villager in the area was suffering from a fox spirit, he asked Zi to go and investigate.' *觇* means to inspect or examine.
  • 至则指狐隐处，令数人随指处击之 - 'Upon arriving, he pointed out where the fox was hiding and ordered several people to strike there.' *隐处* is a classical term for a hidden or concealed place.
  • 即闻狐鸣，毛血交落 - 'Immediately, the fox howled, and its fur and blood fell.' *毛血交落* is a vivid and graphic description of the fox’s death.
  • 自是遂安 - 'From that point on, it was peaceful.' *遂安* is a classical expression of returning to peace or stability.
  • 由是人益异之 - 'From this, people became even more amazed at him.' *益异之* means 'increasingly regarded him as extraordinary.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王 - Refers to the king, likely a local ruler or a member of the aristocracy. The term is used in classical Chinese to denote a ruler, not necessarily an emperor.
  • 仆人 - A servant, likely of high status given the context, as he is able to interact directly with the king.
  • 王孜 - The name of the child, which may be a fictional or symbolic name. *王* is a common surname, and *孜* may imply diligence or effort.
  • 山东王文之子 - 'The son of Wang Wen of Shandong.' *山东* refers to the region of Shandong, and *王文* is likely a historical or fictional figure. The phrase *山东王文* is a full name, possibly a reference to a known historical person or a constructed name for narrative purposes.
  • 鬼狐 - Ghosts and fox spirits are common supernatural beings in Chinese folklore. *狐* (fox) is often associated with shapeshifting and supernatural powers in classical Chinese literature.
  • 田猎 - Hunting, which was a noble pursuit in classical China, often associated with martial prowess and leisure for the aristocracy.
  • 不务生产 - A classical expression meaning not engaged in productive labor, often used to criticize those who do not work or contribute to society.
  • 钳制 - A term used in classical Chinese to describe the act of controlling or restraining someone, often used in the context of governance or discipline.
  • 里中 - Refers to the village or local community, a common term in classical Chinese for a neighborhood or district.
  • 患狐者 - A person who is afflicted by a fox spirit, a common narrative device in classical Chinese ghost stories and supernatural tales.
  • 觇 - To inspect or investigate, often used in the context of supernatural or secret affairs.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 造言生事者 - Someone who spreads rumors or creates trouble.
  • 飞短流长 - Fixed expression meaning 'to spread rumors or slander.'
  • 所不堪受 - 'What one cannot bear' or 'what one finds intolerable.'
  • 偕归同榻 - 'To return together and share a bed,' indicating close intimacy.
  • 快与倾怀 - 'To speak freely and openly,' expressing a deep emotional connection.
  • 病寻愈 - 'The illness soon healed,' indicating recovery.
  • 订为姊妹 - 'To become sisters,' indicating a close bond.
  • 衣服履舄，辄互易着 - 'Clothes and shoes, they would often exchange and wear each other's,' showing their close relationship.
  • 见人来，则隐匿夹幕间 - 'When someone came, they would hide behind the curtains,' indicating secrecy.
  • 积五六月 - 'After several months,' indicating a period of time.
  • 公及夫人颇闻之 - 'The husband and wife heard about it,' indicating that the parents were aware.
  • 两人方对弈 - 'The two were playing chess,' indicating an activity.
  • 夫人掩入 - 'The wife entered quietly,' indicating a stealthy entrance.
  • 谛视 - 'To look closely,' indicating careful observation.
  • 惊曰：“真吾儿友也！” - 'She exclaimed, 'This is truly my daughter's friend!',' indicating recognition.
  • 因谓十一娘：“闺中有良友，我两人所欢，胡不早言？” - 'She then said to Eleven-Niang, 'We have a good friend in the inner chambers, which we both enjoy. Why didn't you tell us earlier?',' indicating a question.
  • 十一娘因达封意 - 'Eleven-Niang then conveyed Fei's intentions,' indicating communication.
  • 伴吾儿，极所忻慰 - 'To be my daughter's companion, it brings great joy,' indicating happiness.
  • 何昧之 - 'Why keep it hidden?,' indicating a question.
  • 封羞晕满颊 - 'Fei blushed deeply,' indicating embarrassment.
  • 默然拈带而已 - 'She silently fidgeted with her belt,' indicating shyness.
  • 夫人去，封乃告别 - 'After the wife left, Fei then bid farewell,' indicating a departure.
  • 十一娘苦留之，乃止 - 'Eleven-Niang tried hard to keep her, and Fei stayed,' indicating persuasion.
  • 一夕，自门外匆忙奔入 - 'One evening, she rushed in from outside,' indicating urgency.
  • 泣曰：“我固谓不可留，今果遭此大辱！” - 'She wept, 'I knew it would be wrong to stay, now I have indeed suffered great humiliation!',' indicating distress.
  • 适出更衣，一少年丈夫，横来相干 - 'Just when she went out to change clothes, a young man suddenly interfered,' indicating an incident.
  • 幸而得逃 - 'Fortunately, she managed to escape,' indicating a fortunate outcome.
  • 如此，复何面目 - 'In this situation, how can I face anyone,' indicating shame.
  • 十一娘细诘形貌 - 'Eleven-Niang carefully asked about his appearance,' indicating investigation.
  • 谢曰：“勿须怪，此妾痴兄。会告夫人，杖责之。” - 'Fei replied, 'There's no need to blame, this is my foolish brother. I will inform the wife, and she will punish him.'', indicating an explanation.
  • 封坚辞欲去 - 'Fei insisted on leaving,' indicating determination.
  • 十一娘请待天曙 - 'Eleven-Niang asked her to wait until dawn,' indicating a request.
  • 舅家咫尺，但须一梯度我过墙耳 - 'My uncle's house is just a short distance away, I only need a ladder to climb over the wall,' indicating a plan.
  • 行半里许，辞谢自去 - 'After walking half a li, she bid farewell and left on her own,' indicating departure.
  • 婢返，十一娘扶床悲惋，如失伉俪 - 'After the maid returned, Eleven-Niang lay in bed, grieving and lamenting, as if she had lost her husband,' indicating deep sorrow.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后数月 - Means 'several months later,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 婢以故至东村 - Means 'the maid, for some reason, went to the eastern village,' where '以故' can mean 'for some reason' or 'because of something.'
  • 暮归 - Means 'returned at dusk,' indicating the time of her return.
  • 遇封女从老妪来 - Means 'met the girl named Feng coming with an old woman,' where '遇' means 'met' and '从老妪来' means 'coming with an old woman.'
  • 婢喜，拜问 - Means 'the maid was happy and bowed to ask,' where '拜问' means 'to bow and ask.'
  • 封亦恻恻 - Means 'Feng was also sad,' where '恻恻' describes a feeling of sadness or sympathy.
  • 讯十一娘兴居 - Means 'inquired about the well-being of Lady Shi,' where '讯' means 'to inquire' and '兴居' means 'well-being' or 'health.'
  • 婢捉袂曰 - Means 'the maid grabbed her sleeve and said,' where '捉袂' means 'grabbed her sleeve.'
  • 三姑过我 - Means 'the three aunts have come to visit me,' where '三姑' refers to the aunts and '过我' means 'have come to visit me.'
  • 我家姑姑盼欲死 - Means 'my aunt has been waiting anxiously,' where '盼欲死' is an idiom meaning 'has been waiting anxiously.'
  • 我亦思之 - Means 'I have missed her too,' where '思之' means 'have missed her.'
  • 但不乐使家人知 - Means 'but I don't want the family to know,' where '不乐' means 'don't want' and '使家人知' means 'let the family know.'
  • 归启园门，我自至 - Means 'go back and open the garden gate, I will come myself,' where '启园门' means 'open the garden gate' and '我自至' means 'I will come myself.'
  • 相见，各道间阔 - Means 'they met, and each recounted their separation,' where '各道间阔' means 'each recounted their separation.'
  • 绵绵不寐 - Means 'they talked late into the night,' where '绵绵' means 'long' and '不寐' means 'didn't sleep.'
  • 视婢子眠熟，乃起 - Means 'when they saw the maid's child was asleep, they got up,' where '视婢子眠熟' means 'saw the maid's child was asleep' and '乃起' means 'got up.'
  • 移与十一娘同枕 - Means 'they moved to share a pillow with Lady Shi,' where '移与十一娘同枕' means 'moved to share a pillow with Lady Shi.'
  • 私语曰 - Means 'whispered and said,' where '私语' means 'whispered.'
  • 妾固知娘子未字 - Means 'I already knew that Lady Shi had not yet been married,' where '妾固知' means 'I already knew' and '未字' means 'had not yet been married.'
  • 以才色门第 - Means 'because of her talent, beauty, and family background,' where '才色门第' means 'talent, beauty, and family background.'
  • 何患无贵介婿 - Means 'there is no need to worry about not finding a noble husband,' where '何患' means 'there is no need to worry' and '贵介婿' means 'noble husband.'
  • 然绔袴儿敖不足数 - Means 'but those wealthy and arrogant boys are not worth counting,' where '然' means 'but,' '绔袴儿' means 'wealthy and arrogant boys,' and '敖不足数' means 'are not worth counting.'
  • 如欲得佳偶，请无以贫富论 - Means 'if you want to find a good match, please do not consider wealth and poverty,' where '如欲得佳偶' means 'if you want to find a good match' and '请无以贫富论' means 'please do not consider wealth and poverty.'
  • 十一娘然之 - Means 'Lady Shi agreed,' where '然之' means 'agreed.'
  • 旧年邂逅处，今复作道场 - Means 'the place where we met last year, now they are holding a ritual again,' where '旧年邂逅处' means 'the place where we met last year' and '今复作道场' means 'now they are holding a ritual again.'
  • 明日再烦一往，当令见一如意郎君 - Means 'please come again tomorrow, and I will arrange for you to meet a suitable young man,' where '明日再烦一往' means 'please come again tomorrow' and '当令见一如意郎君' means 'I will arrange for you to meet a suitable young man.'
  • 妾少读相人书 - Means 'I have read books on physiognomy since I was young,' where '妾少读' means 'I have read since I was young' and '相人书' means 'books on physiognomy.'
  • 颇不参差 - Means 'it is quite accurate,' where '颇不参差' means 'quite accurate.'
  • 昧爽封即去 - Means 'Feng left at dawn,' where '昧爽' means 'at dawn' and '封即去' means 'Feng left.'
  • 约俟兰若 - Means 'she agreed to wait at the temple,' where '约俟' means 'agreed to wait' and '兰若' means 'temple.'
  • 十一娘果往，封已先在 - Means 'Lady Shi went as agreed, and Feng was already there,' where '果往' means 'went as agreed' and '封已先在' means 'Feng was already there.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 眺览一周 - Phrase meaning 'to look around for a full circle' or 'to survey the surroundings completely'; implies a thorough observation or inspection.
  • 便邀同车 - Concise expression meaning 'then invited to share a carriage'; common in Classical Chinese to express joining someone in a journey.
  • 容仪俊伟 - Compound phrase meaning 'appearance and bearing are handsome and impressive'; used to describe someone's striking and noble appearance.
  • 翰苑才 - Term of honor referring to a talented individual in the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious imperial court institution.
  • 不以为可 - Literally 'not to consider it acceptable'; in context, means 'not to find it suitable' or 'not to think it acceptable.'
  • 苟长贫贱 - Conditional phrase meaning 'if he remains poor and lowly'; '苟' introduces a hypothetical condition.
  • 抉眸子 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to pluck out one's eyes'; hyperbolic way of saying one would sacrifice something precious to avoid a mistake.
  • 不复相天下士 - Literally 'will no longer associate with men of the world'; means 'will no longer consider or associate with outstanding talents.'
  • 魔劫未消 - Term from Buddhist or Daoist cosmology meaning 'the karmic obstacles or trials have not yet passed.'
  • 矫命赠之 - Phrase meaning 'to give it under false authority' or 'to gift it under the pretense of an order'; suggests an act done in the name of someone else.
  • 方谋更商 - Phrase meaning 'was just about to deliberate further' or 'was in the process of considering more carefully.'

  • 同里孟安仁 - Name with a location: '同里' (same village or hometown) and the name '孟安仁'; indicates the man is a local from the same village or region as the speaker.
  • 魔劫 - Term from Buddhist or Daoist cosmology referring to karmic obstacles or trials; used in classical Chinese fiction to explain supernatural or fateful events.
  • 姻缘已动 - Phrase from Chinese cosmology and literature meaning 'the karmic bond of marriage has already been set in motion'; suggests a romantic or marital connection is fated.
  • 金凤钗 - A type of ornamental hairpin or jewelry; often used as a symbol of status, beauty, or a gift in classical Chinese literature; may carry symbolic or magical significance.

  • 封潜 - Proper noun, the name of another character.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 十八猶未聘 - Literally 'eighteen and still not married,' used to describe someone who is unmarried at an older age, implying a delay in marriage.
  • 忽睹兩艷 - Literally 'suddenly saw two beauties,' used to describe an unexpected encounter with two attractive women.
  • 歸涉冥想 - Literally 'returned to deep thought,' used to describe a state of profound contemplation after an event.
  • 遽前擁抱 - Literally 'rushed forward to embrace,' used to describe an impulsive and affectionate action.
  • 妾非毛遂，乃曹丘生 - A play on words. '毛遂' (Mao Sui) is a historical figure known for his self-recommendation, while '曹丘生' (Cao Qiu Sheng) is a reference to a man who was known for his eloquence and ability to persuade others. Here, it means 'I am not like Mao Sui, but rather like Cao Qiu Sheng.'
  • 請倩冰 - Literally 'please ask for an ice maker,' a metaphorical expression for asking for a matchmaker.
  • 勞眷注如此 - Literally 'so much care and attention,' used to express gratitude for someone's concern and affection.
  • 矢曰 - Literally 'swore,' used to describe a solemn promise or declaration.
  • 只須以死矢之 - Literally 'must swear by death,' used to express a strong commitment or vow.
  • 浼鄰媪詣范夫人 - Literally 'asked a neighbor woman to go to Mrs. Fan,' used to describe a request made through an intermediary.
  • 夫人貧之 - Literally 'the lady found him poor,' used to describe a negative reaction based on someone's financial status.
  • 心失所望 - Literally 'lost hope,' used to describe disappointment.
  • 深恨封之誤己也 - Literally 'deeply resented being deceived by Feeng,' used to express strong feelings of betrayal.
  • 金釵難返 - Literally 'the golden hairpin cannot be returned,' used to describe a situation where a gift cannot be easily given back.
